summary.intent: Address 9 Codex review findings on the carousel component covering auto-play timer lifecycle bugs and slides_per_view > 1 semantics gaps.
summary.outcome: All 9 findings fixed in impl and spec; 74 logic tests + 22 snapshots pass; xci-fast gate green; changes committed and pushed.

repo: Carousel spec lives at spec/components/layout/carousel.md and must be kept in sync with the Rust impl — both were updated together.
The project uses a `cargo xci-fast` pre-push gate that runs lib tests, snapshots, spec validation, and snippet compilation.
Codex reviews are tracked as GitHub PR threads; the workflow polls for reactions/threads and then replies per-thread after the fix lands.
Snapshot tests live alongside logic tests in crates/ars-components/src/layout/carousel/mod.rs and capture rendered HTML attribute output.

code.finding: Added visible_count() and last_index() helpers to CarouselContext to encapsulate slides_per_view arithmetic used by clamp_index and can_go_next.
Introduced navigate_to() helper on Machine to DRY manual-navigation logic shared across GoToSlide, PointerUp, and FocusSlide transition arms.
default_index is now clamped to last_index() at init to prevent out-of-bounds starting positions when slides_per_view > 1.
Visible-but-not-current slides in multi-view mode must have ars-active=false and neither aria-hidden nor inert — confirmed via new snapshot test.
Two existing tests were asserting old buggy behavior (timer not cancelled on manual nav, Next enabled past last full page) and required updating to new contract.
Spec needed navigate_to helper added, AutoPlayResume un-stop semantics clarified, PointerUp resume/stop logic updated, and item_group live-region attribute documented.

workflow: Codex review findings were verified manually against the code before fixing — all 9 were confirmed correct, none dismissed.
Per-thread Codex replies were prepared after the fix but held until xci-fast passed, ensuring replies only go out after CI is green.
Spec edits required multiple read-then-edit cycles because earlier edits shifted line numbers and left dangling code — always re-read the region after each edit.

friction: Spec edits required repeated read-then-edit cycles; one edit left dangling code that needed a follow-up fix, caused by shifted line numbers after prior edits.
The xci-fast gate was run while preparing Codex replies in parallel to avoid blocking on CI — required careful sequencing to ensure replies only sent after green.
open_items: Per-thread Codex reply content was prepared but the actual posting step is not shown as completed in the transcript — may still be pending.

Multi-view carousel semantics (visible_count, last_index, inert behavior) are now implemented but could benefit from additional property-based tests covering edge cases like slides_per_view >= total slides.
